conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm looking to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you determine if you can claim Child Benefit for your child.

**What is your child's name and age?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child's name is Alex, and they're 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 18 years old, their eligibility may depend on whether they're in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as A-levels, T-levels, Scottish Highers, or other approved non-advanced courses)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. They finished school but haven't started anything else yet.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that information. Let me also ask: **Are you responsible for Alex? This means do they live with you, or do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit would pay towards looking after them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just to complete my assessment, I need to ask:

**Are you or Alex currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Complete

Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: While Alex lives with you in the UK and you are responsible for them, Child Benefit eligibility for young people aged 16-19 requires them to be in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and is not currently enrolled in any education or training program, they don't meet the eligibility criteria.

**However**, if Alex decides to enroll in approved education or training in the future (such as A-levels, T-levels, Scottish Highers, or other approved non-advanced courses), you may become eligible to claim Child Benefit again, up until the week after their 20th birthday or when they leave education/training - whichever comes first.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and is not currently in approved education or training. According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can only get Child Benefit for a young person aged 16 to 19 if they are in approved education or training (such as A-levels, T-levels, or other approved non-advanced courses). Since Alex has finished school and hasn't started any education or training program, they do not meet the criteria for Child Benefit eligibility at age 18. While the claimant is responsible for Alex (who lives with them) and they both live in the UK, the age and education requirement is not satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex (age 18), who lives with them in the UK. However, Alex is not eligible for Child Benefit because they are 18 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit can only be claimed for young people aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training.
